trade.summary: China is the UK's second-largest import source. The UK runs a very large trade deficit with China. UK exports machinery, pharmaceuticals, vehicles, and financial services. China exports electronics, clothing, machinery, and consumer goods. Relations have been complicated by Huawei exclusion from UK 5G network and other technology controls.
disputes: Chinese sanctions on UK MPs and institutions (2021)

diplomatic.summary: Relations have deteriorated sharply from the 'Golden Era' of 2015-2016. The UK has become one of China's most vocal critics in Europe, sanctioning Chinese officials over Xinjiang, condemning erosion of Hong Kong autonomy, and designating Confucius Institutes a security risk. China sanctioned UK MPs. The UK-Australia-US AUKUS pact and Five Eyes intelligence alliance align UK firmly against Chinese interests.
recent_incidents: UK banned Huawei from 5G networks (2020-2027 phase-out)
UK imposed Xinjiang sanctions; China retaliated against UK MPs
UK National Security Act targets Chinese interference operations
Chinese espionage cases in UK Parliament (2024)

military.summary: Firmly in opposing alignments. UK is a core NATO member, Five Eyes member, and AUKUS partner. China views UK as aligned against Chinese interests. UK has sent Royal Navy vessels to South China Sea and Taiwan Strait. No arms transfers.
incidents: UK Royal Navy freedom of navigation in South China Sea
UK criticized Chinese military exercises near Taiwan
UK charged individuals with espionage for China (2024)

financial.summary: Declining but still significant financial ties. London is a major offshore RMB center but China's share of UK investment is declining. UK has increased scrutiny of Chinese FDI. Chinese companies (e.g., CATL, Huawei) have UK operations. UK pension funds have reduced China exposure.

energy.summary: UK is reviewing Chinese involvement in nuclear power (CGN stake in Hinkley Point C, Bradwell project effectively shelved). UK imports Chinese solar panels. Limited direct energy trade. UK reducing strategic energy dependence on China.

scientific.summary: Scientific cooperation is under significant review. UK universities have been under government pressure to scrutinize Chinese partnerships for security risks. Some partnerships terminated. Joint publications remain high but institutional links are being reviewed under National Security provisions.
